titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the United Campus Christian Fellowship Records
titlestmt.author: Mary N.S. Richardson and Noemi Palomares

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 22:18

did.abstract: The United Campus Christian Fellowship was a national, Protestant, ecumenical organization that existed between the years 1960-1968.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Princeton Theological Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: United Campus Christian Fellowship Records (RG 254), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The United Campus Christian Fellowship was a national, Protestant, ecumenical student movement that existed between 1960-1968. The UCCF was a merger between the student work departments of the Disciples of Christ, the Presbyterian Church in U.S.A., Evangelical United Brethren, and the United Church of Christ. Once merged, the UCCF was comprised of regional member movements and local groups. UCCF was also a participating member of the National Student Christian Federation and later the University Christian Movement. In 1968, the UCCF voted to dissolve as the UCM had taken its place as the national Christian student movement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files include correspondence, reports, and publications of regional UCCF groups and related organizations.

did.unittitle: K.I.M.O.N.A (Kansas, Iowa, Missouri, Oklahoma, Nebraska, Arkansas)
Pacific Northwest - Wash, Oregon, Montana, N. Idaho
University Christian Movement Misc Folder -Richard Van Voorhis file -Publication Secretary -Mostly Field Dept related 1966
UCM 66-67 -Misc File -Richard Van Voorhis UCM File -Separated printed materials from corresp.
